message:
chore(beads): update ft-1i2ge.8 with proptest coverage landing note

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -296,7 +296,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.7.5","title":"[mission][G5] Production go/no-go decision package","description":"Assemble final evidence package and decision rubric for production enablement.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Package includes safety, reliability, performance, and operator-readiness evidence.
2. Decision rubric yields clear go/no-go outcome.
3. Follow-up items are captured as explicit beads.
4. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests, executable e2e test scripts, and detailed structured logs/artifacts proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
5. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_7_5.sh and detailed structured logs under tests/e2e/logs/ft_1i2ge_7_5_*.jsonl proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
6. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Final decision should be principled and auditable, not ,"type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.7.6","title":"[mission][G6] Long-haul soak, chaos burn-in, and evidence bundle generation","description":"Run sustained multi-hour mission soak and chaos campaigns, then publish reproducible evidence bundles.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Long-haul soak runs at target swarm scale and includes tx-heavy workloads (high-rate `tx run`, induced rollback storms, resume-after-restart) with deterministic chaos injections.
2. Unit tests validate soak/chaos campaign configuration parsing, run orchestration, tx workload generators, and evidence bundling logic.
3. E2E scripts `tests/e2e/test_mission_soak.sh` and `tests/e2e/test_mission_chaos.sh` execute sustained and adversarial campaigns with fixed seeds.
4. Campaign evidence is emitted to `tests/e2e/logs/mission_soak_*.jsonl` and `tests/e2e/logs/mission_chaos_*.jsonl` with tx_id/correlation_id linkage for timeline reconstruction.
5. Assertions verify no unbounded degradation, no unsafe dispatch escalation, and deterministic recovery behavior after failures.
6. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests, executable e2e test scripts, and detailed structured logs/artifacts proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
7. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"This closes the gap between synthetic tests and production reality by proving operational durability.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:04:15.145994Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:19:39.948795Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T08:19:39.948771Z","close_reason":"Implemented mission soak+chaos e2e harnesses, wired ft_1i2ge_7_6 scenario into scripts/e2e_test.sh, and generated deterministic evidence bundle at docs/metrics/mission_soak_chaos_evidence.json with linked logs mission_soak_*.jsonl and mission_chaos_*.jsonl (all heavy cargo via rch exec).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","e2e-tests","hardening","integration-tests","logging","mission","performance","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.7.7","title":"[mission][G7] Operator takeover game-days and emergency-control certification","description":"Run structured operator takeover drills and certify emergency-control readiness before production go/no-go.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Game-day suite covers emergency stop, manual takeover, degraded-mode operation, controlled recovery, and tx-interrupted takeover paths under realistic multi-agent load.
2. Unit tests validate takeover state transitions, control-plane authority checks, tx override/rollback safety boundaries, and operator-action audit integrity.
3. E2E script `tests/e2e/test_mission_game_day.sh` executes repeatable drills with structured scenario manifests and deterministic seeds.
4. Drill timelines, decision traces, and operator-action audits are emitted to `tests/e2e/logs/mission_game_day_*.jsonl` with tx_id/correlation_id linkage where applicable.
5. Certification criteria include maximum takeover latency, correctness of emergency controls, and explicit rollback readiness evidence.
6. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests, executable e2e test scripts, and detailed structured logs/artifacts proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
7. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Production readiness requires demonstrated human takeover competence, not just autonomous-path correctness. This bead certifies that operators can recover control quickly and safely in worst-case conditions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBrook","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:28:09.856440Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:24:43.784823Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T08:24:43.784744Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","e2e-tests","hardening","integration-tests","logging","mission","operations","quality-gate","safety","test-coverage","test-logging","tests","user-impact","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.7.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-1i2ge.8","title":"[mission][track] Transactional multi-pane execution substrate (ft robot tx)","description":"## Objective
Add a first-class **transactional execution substrate** to mission/autopilot so multi-pane swarm actions are atomic, reversible, and auditable.

This track introduces a dedicated `ft robot tx` command family and runtime semantics for:
- `ft robot tx plan`: compile dependency graph, preconditions, and compensations
- `ft robot tx run`: execute with prepare/commit phases and safety gates
- `ft robot tx rollback`: auto or operator-triggered compensation on partial failure
- `ft robot tx show <id>`: inspect intent ledger, receipts, and causal timeline

## Why this exists
Mission-level orchestration currently optimizes *what* to do next. This track optimizes *how* risky multi-step execution happens under failure.

Without transaction semantics, partial success causes inconsistent swarm state:
- Some panes executed, others did not
- Reservations/approvals partially consumed
- Recovery requires manual forensics

This track prevents partial-failure chaos by introducing deterministic execution boundaries and compensation.

## Scope
1. Transaction domain model and state machine.
2. Intent ledger + causal receipts.
3. Plan compiler for action/precondition/compensation DAGs.
4. Prepare/commit runtime with policy + reservation + approval gates.
5. Compensation/rollback runtime with deterministic replay semantics.
6. Idempotency and resume invariants.
7. CLI/Robot/MCP tx surfaces.
8. Comprehensive unit/property/concurrency/e2e/chaos validation and structured logging.

## Non-goals
- Replacing mission planner ranking logic (handled in existing B-track).
- Bypassing policy/approval constraints for speed.
- Introducing hidden side channels that avoid audit logging.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. `ft robot tx plan/run/rollback/show` exists with stable envelopes and deterministic behavior for identical inputs.
2. Prepare phase enforces policy, reservation, and approval checks before any commit-phase side effects.
3. Commit phase provides explicit atomicity boundaries (all committed effects accounted for, or compensation path triggered with reason codes).
4. Compensation engine can automatically rollback partial executions with deterministic, auditable compensating actions.
5. Intent ledger stores full provenance: intent, preconditions, execution decisions, receipts, compensation decisions, and final outcome.
6. Unit tests cover happy path, edge cases, and failure taxonomy branches for every transaction state transition.
7. Property/concurrency tests validate idempotency, retry safety, and no-double-commit/no-double-compensation invariants under interleavings.
8. End-to-end scripts cover nominal, partial failure, policy denial, reservation conflict, timeout, retry, resume, and operator-forced rollback scenarios.
9. Structured logs/events are rich enough to reconstruct full tx timelines and support replay/forensics without consulting source.
10. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_track.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_track_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
11. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Design principles:
- Safety before throughput: no execution path may bypass policy/reservation/approval gates.
- Determinism over cleverness: repeated runs with identical artifacts should produce identical decisions.
- Explainability as a product feature: every transition carries reason codes + remediation hints.
- Reversible-by-default: if commit cannot safely complete, compensation must be first-class and observable.

Program interaction:
- Complements mission planning track by hardening execution semantics.
- Feeds replay/counterfactual initiatives via richer receipt artifacts.
- Tightens ARS/autopilot safety envelope by reducing blast radius of imperfect plans.

Optimization directive:
- Reuse existing mission subsystems wherever possible; this track adds tx-specific semantics and contracts rather than duplicating generic planner/orchestration/safety code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:29.194153Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-14T18:42:47.900058Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-mail","autopilot","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mission","orchestration","policy","property-tests","quality-gate","robot-mode","rollback","safety","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Dependency overlay (execution sequence):\nPhase H0 contracts: H1 -> H2.\nPhase H1 planning: H3 (depends on existing B-track readiness/scoring context).\nPhase H2 safety preflight: H4 (depends on existing D-track guardrails).\nPhase H3 execution: H5.\nPhase H4 recovery: H6 + H7.\nPhase H5 interfaces + observability: H8 + H9.\nPhase H6 proof: H10 -> H11 -> H12.\nGlobal quality bar: each phase requires explicit unit/property/integration/e2e evidence + rich logging artifacts before progressing.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:56Z"},{"id":133,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Transactional track architecture note (self-contained):\n- Problem: multi-pane orchestration currently lacks explicit atomicity + compensation semantics, causing partial-failure inconsistency and expensive manual recovery.\n- Core design: tx plan -> prepare -> commit -> compensate/rollback, backed by durable intent ledger + causal receipts.\n- Safety doctrine: no commit before prepare; no prepare bypass of policy/reservation/approval; no silent compensation.\n- Observability doctrine: every transition emits structured logs and reason codes sufficient for replay/forensics without source inspection.\n- Product outcome: safer autonomy with reversible execution under failure.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:56Z"},{"id":1643,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"RainyHill","text":"Support slice landed (tx interface harness hardening, 2026-02-28): updated tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh to harden rch execution diagnostics and remote temp handling.\n\nChanges:\n1) Added remote TMPDIR override for rch cargo invocations (REMOTE_TMPDIR bound to run-specific target dir) for both preflight smoke and tx command executions.\n2) Added explicit disk-exhaustion taxonomy (RCH-E102) in both smoke and run command failure paths when stderr/stdout contains 'No space left on device'.\n3) Added explicit worker capability taxonomy (RCH-E103) and daemon-unreachable taxonomy (RCH-E104) for local-fallback/nonzero smoke paths and command fallback paths.\n4) Preserved fail-closed policy: any '[RCH] local' still aborts immediately; now with richer reason_code/error_code emission.\n\nValidation:\n- bash -n tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh ✅\n- shellcheck tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh ✅\n- E2E runs:\n  - tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_171447.jsonl (show_nominal failed with local fallback marker: no workers with Rust installed)\n  - tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_171607.jsonl (run started; manually terminated during long remote compile while monitoring)\n\nStatus:\n- Harness now emits deterministic failure taxonomy for disk/daemon/worker-capability classes and keeps strict offload-only guardrails.\n- Remaining blocker appears environment-side rch worker capability/availability drift at execution time, not silent harness ambiguity.","created_at":"2026-02-28T22:19:26Z"},{"id":1649,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"RainyHill","text":"Follow-up tx interface harness hardening (2026-02-28): added remote disk-capacity preflight in tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh with structured pass/fail telemetry before tx command execution (artifacts: mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_174407.*, mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_174722.*). Added retry remediation for RCH-E104 path: one-shot ● Restarting RCH daemon...

○ Daemon is not running (socket not found)
Starting RCH daemon...
⚠ Daemon process started but socket not found.
  ● Check logs with: rch daemon logs + retry preflight artifacts before second attempt. Validation: bash -n + shellcheck passed. E2E outcomes: (1) 174407 and 174722 runs show preflight_rch_disk_capacity=passed but show_nominal still fails with RCH-E104 daemon timeout/local fallback; (2) 175036 run records daemon restart attempt () but still falls back local and then local compile hit No space left on device due host disk pressure. Local filesystem currently ~112Mi free on /System/Volumes/Data, making further compile/e2e validation unreliable until disk is freed.","created_at":"2026-02-28T22:53:30Z"},{"id":1651,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"RainyHill","text":"Correction: previous comment had shell interpolation artifacts. Hardening now includes remote disk preflight and an E104 daemon-restart retry path in tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh. Validation passed for bash -n and shellcheck. E2E artifacts: mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_174407.jsonl, mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_174722.jsonl, mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_175036.jsonl. 174407 and 174722 show preflight_rch_disk_capacity passed but show_nominal failed with RCH-E104 daemon timeout fallback; 175036 logged daemon restart but still failed with RCH-E104 and then local compile ENOSPC after fallback. Current blocker: host disk is nearly full (~112Mi free on /System/Volumes/Data), so further compile/e2e validation is unreliable until space is reclaimed.","created_at":"2026-02-28T22:53:52Z"},{"id":2007,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"PlumDeer","text":"Reopened on 2026-03-08 after bv --robot-alerts flagged this issue as stale in_progress with 7+ days of inactivity and no recent bead-thread progress. Clearing the stale claim so it can be triaged again by active agents.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:01:26Z"},{"id":2069,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"BrightPond","text":"Claiming a bounded support slice on the tx track. Focus: eliminate CLI/MCP parity drift by moving synthetic tx helper builders (prepare gate inputs, commit inputs, compensation inputs, synthetic prior-commit report) onto shared plan-layer helpers and repoint both surfaces there. Explicitly staying out of the blocked H8 e2e harness file tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh unless coordinated.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:24:14Z"},{"id":2070,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"BrightPond","text":"Landed bounded tx parity slice on 2026-03-10: moved the synthetic tx helper builders out of surface-specific code and into crates/frankenterm-core/src/plan.rs (mission_tx_prepare_gate_inputs, mission_tx_commit_step_inputs, mission_tx_compensation_inputs, mission_tx_synthetic_commit_report). Repointed the CLI robot tx surface in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and the MCP surface import path in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs to the shared helpers. Removed the duplicate helper implementations from crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_missions.rs and kept parity coverage by updating its tests to exercise the shared plan-layer helpers; also added direct shared-helper tests in plan.rs. Local formatting verification passed with rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on the four touched files. Remote validation is currently infra-blocked: rch workers probe --all --json reports all 8 workers connection_failed with RCH-E100, and rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets fell back to local execution after SSH failure, so I terminated the fallback to respect the repo offload-only rule. rch exec -- cargo fmt --check is also failing on unrelated pre-existing workspace formatting drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:32:21Z"},{"id":2341,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"TanSummit claiming ft-1i2ge.8: adding proptest coverage for tx types (tx_execution, tx_idempotency, tx_observability, tx_plan_compiler) and edge case unit tests. Non-overlapping with existing code — tests only.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:42:47Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-1i2ge.8","title":"[mission][track] Transactional multi-pane execution substrate (ft robot tx)","description":"## Objective
Add a first-class **transactional execution substrate** to mission/autopilot so multi-pane swarm actions are atomic, reversible, and auditable.

This track introduces a dedicated `ft robot tx` command family and runtime semantics for:
- `ft robot tx plan`: compile dependency graph, preconditions, and compensations
- `ft robot tx run`: execute with prepare/commit phases and safety gates
- `ft robot tx rollback`: auto or operator-triggered compensation on partial failure
- `ft robot tx show <id>`: inspect intent ledger, receipts, and causal timeline

## Why this exists
Mission-level orchestration currently optimizes *what* to do next. This track optimizes *how* risky multi-step execution happens under failure.

Without transaction semantics, partial success causes inconsistent swarm state:
- Some panes executed, others did not
- Reservations/approvals partially consumed
- Recovery requires manual forensics

This track prevents partial-failure chaos by introducing deterministic execution boundaries and compensation.

## Scope
1. Transaction domain model and state machine.
2. Intent ledger + causal receipts.
3. Plan compiler for action/precondition/compensation DAGs.
4. Prepare/commit runtime with policy + reservation + approval gates.
5. Compensation/rollback runtime with deterministic replay semantics.
6. Idempotency and resume invariants.
7. CLI/Robot/MCP tx surfaces.
8. Comprehensive unit/property/concurrency/e2e/chaos validation and structured logging.

## Non-goals
- Replacing mission planner ranking logic (handled in existing B-track).
- Bypassing policy/approval constraints for speed.
- Introducing hidden side channels that avoid audit logging.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. `ft robot tx plan/run/rollback/show` exists with stable envelopes and deterministic behavior for identical inputs.
2. Prepare phase enforces policy, reservation, and approval checks before any commit-phase side effects.
3. Commit phase provides explicit atomicity boundaries (all committed effects accounted for, or compensation path triggered with reason codes).
4. Compensation engine can automatically rollback partial executions with deterministic, auditable compensating actions.
5. Intent ledger stores full provenance: intent, preconditions, execution decisions, receipts, compensation decisions, and final outcome.
6. Unit tests cover happy path, edge cases, and failure taxonomy branches for every transaction state transition.
7. Property/concurrency tests validate idempotency, retry safety, and no-double-commit/no-double-compensation invariants under interleavings.
8. End-to-end scripts cover nominal, partial failure, policy denial, reservation conflict, timeout, retry, resume, and operator-forced rollback scenarios.
9. Structured logs/events are rich enough to reconstruct full tx timelines and support replay/forensics without consulting source.
10. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_track.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_track_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
11. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Design principles:
- Safety before throughput: no execution path may bypass policy/reservation/approval gates.
- Determinism over cleverness: repeated runs with identical artifacts should produce identical decisions.
- Explainability as a product feature: every transition carries reason codes + remediation hints.
- Reversible-by-default: if commit cannot safely complete, compensation must be first-class and observable.

Program interaction:
- Complements mission planning track by hardening execution semantics.
- Feeds replay/counterfactual initiatives via richer receipt artifacts.
- Tightens ARS/autopilot safety envelope by reducing blast radius of imperfect plans.

Optimization directive:
- Reuse existing mission subsystems wherever possible; this track adds tx-specific semantics and contracts rather than duplicating generic planner/orchestration/safety code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:29.194153Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:01:17.464661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-mail","autopilot","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mission","orchestration","policy","property-tests","quality-gate","robot-mode","rollback","safety","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Dependency overlay (execution sequence):\nPhase H0 contracts: H1 -> H2.\nPhase H1 planning: H3 (depends on existing B-track readiness/scoring context).\nPhase H2 safety preflight: H4 (depends on existing D-track guardrails).\nPhase H3 execution: H5.\nPhase H4 recovery: H6 + H7.\nPhase H5 interfaces + observability: H8 + H9.\nPhase H6 proof: H10 -> H11 -> H12.\nGlobal quality bar: each phase requires explicit unit/property/integration/e2e evidence + rich logging artifacts before progressing.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:56Z"},{"id":133,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Transactional track architecture note (self-contained):\n- Problem: multi-pane orchestration currently lacks explicit atomicity + compensation semantics, causing partial-failure inconsistency and expensive manual recovery.\n- Core design: tx plan -> prepare -> commit -> compensate/rollback, backed by durable intent ledger + causal receipts.\n- Safety doctrine: no commit before prepare; no prepare bypass of policy/reservation/approval; no silent compensation.\n- Observability doctrine: every transition emits structured logs and reason codes sufficient for replay/forensics without source inspection.\n- Product outcome: safer autonomy with reversible execution under failure.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:56Z"},{"id":1643,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","author":"RainyHill","text":"Support slice landed (tx interface harness hardening, 2026-02-28): updated tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh to harden rch execution diagnostics and remote temp handling.\n\nChanges:\n1) Added remote TMPDIR override for rch cargo invocations (REMOTE_TMPDIR bound to run-specific target dir) for both preflight smoke and tx command executions.\n2) Added explicit disk-exhaustion taxonomy (RCH-E102) in both smoke and run command failure paths when stderr/stdout contains 'No space left on device'.\n3) Added explicit worker capability taxonomy (RCH-E103) and daemon-unreachable taxonomy (RCH-E104) for local-fallback/nonzero smoke paths and command fallback paths.\n4) Preserved fail-closed policy: any '[RCH] local' still aborts immediately;  {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","title":"[mission][H1] Transaction domain contract, lifecycle state machine, and invariants","description":"## Objective
Define the transaction contract (nouns, lifecycle, invariants, failure semantics) as executable architecture.

## Scope
- Canonical entities: `TxIntent`, `TxPlan`, `TxStep`, `TxPrecondition`, `TxCompensation`, `TxReceipt`, `TxOutcome`.
- Lifecycle states: Draft -> Planned -> Prepared -> Committing -> Committed OR Compensating -> RolledBack/Failed.
- Failure taxonomy expansion: plan_invalid, precondition_failed, prepare_denied, prepare_timeout, commit_partial, compensation_failed, rollback_forced, etc.
- Invariant catalog: no-commit-before-prepare, no-double-commit, no-compensate-without-failed-commit, monotonic receipt ordering.

## Deliverables
- Schema/state-machine spec embedded in code-facing contract docs.
- Transition table with legal/illegal transitions.
- Machine-readable reason-code map with retryability + operator guidance.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Contract defines all transaction entities, IDs/provenance requirements, and state transitions unambiguously.
2. Illegal transition matrix is explicit and testable.
3. Failure taxonomy includes retryability classification and operator hint/remediation fields.
4. Unit tests validate all legal and illegal transitions.
5. Property tests prove key invariants (single terminal outcome, monotonic receipts, no duplicate commit/compensation markers).
6. Structured logs for transition events include tx_id, state_from, state_to, reason_code, and correlation_id.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_contract.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_contract_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: this bead is the constitutional layer for tx runtime; every downstream implementation bead should reference this contract rather than redefining semantics ad hoc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:29.735118Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-24T16:02:28.350101Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T16:02:28.350086Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","e2e-tests","errors","integration-tests","logging","mission","property-tests","quality-gate","state-machine","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1296,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","author":"BoldMeadow","text":"Claimed by BoldMeadow. Starting implementation of transaction contract + lifecycle invariants in mission transaction domain. Coordination note: MCP Agent Mail registration/inbox works, but mail send currently fails with SQLite constraint errors in this environment; using contact requests + bead comments for progress trace until mail write path recovers.","created_at":"2026-02-24T04:12:27Z"},{"id":1298,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","author":"BoldMeadow","text":"Progress update: implemented transaction domain contract in crates/frankenterm-core/src/plan.rs with canonical entities (TxIntent/TxPlan/TxStep/TxPrecondition/TxCompensation/TxReceipt/TxOutcome), lifecycle transition matrix, machine-readable failure taxonomy (reason_code/error_code/retryability/hints), structured transition log contract, and invariant validation. Added coverage: nominal path, failure injections, recovery-path acceptance, and proptest invariant checks (single terminal outcome, monotonic receipts, duplicate commit markers, compensation marker rule). Added required E2E harness at tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_contract.sh producing tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_contract_*.jsonl with required fields (timestamp/component/scenario_id/correlation_id/decision_path/input_summary/outcome/reason_code/error_code/artifact_path). Validation blocker: rch offload workers currently unreachable in this environment (rch workers probe --all fails all 8 with RCH-E100), so full cargo check/clippy/test verification is pending worker connectivity recovery.","created_at":"2026-02-24T04:24:20Z"},{"id":1403,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RusticGate: Verified BoldMeadow's implementation locally (no rch needed). All 14 mission_tx_contract tests pass: happy path, recovery with compensation, illegal transition rejection, failure taxonomy uniqueness, non-monotonic receipt rejection, transition log contract, and 4 property tests. Compilation clean with only warnings from unrelated modules.","created_at":"2026-02-24T16:01:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.10","title":"[mission][H10] Unit/property/concurrency correctness suite for tx semantics","description":"## Objective
Create exhaustive correctness test harnesses for transaction semantics at unit/property/concurrency levels.

## Scope
- State machine transition tests and reason-code assertions.
- Invariant property tests for commit/compensation semantics.
- Concurrency stress tests for duplicate submissions, interleavings, and restart races.
- Deterministic seed logging for reproducible failures.

## Deliverables
- Unit/property/concurrency test suites integrated into CI.
- Test artifact conventions and deterministic repro scripts.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Comprehensive unit tests cover all happy/error branches in plan, prepare, commit, compensate, rollback, and resume paths, including reason-code and terminal-state assertions.
2. Property tests validate invariants: no invalid terminal combinations, monotonic causal ordering, idempotent replay behavior, and compensation completeness guarantees.
3. Concurrency tests validate no duplicate effects under interleavings, duplicate submissions, reservation races, approval timing races, and restart races.
4. E2E scripts `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_correctness.sh` and `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_concurrency.sh` execute deterministic correctness and race scenarios with fixed seeds.
5. Structured logs are written under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_correctness_*.jsonl` and `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_concurrency_*.jsonl` with required fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, tx_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path, seed.
6. CI surfaces clear failure diagnostics with reproducible rerun commands and emits deterministic replay manifests mapping scenario -> fixtures/seeds -> expected invariants.","notes":"Future-self note: this bead is the semantic safety net; treat flaky nondeterministic tests as defects, not noise.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:34.366674Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:42:48.411014Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:42:48.410999Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mission","property-tests","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.10","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.10","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1520,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.10","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RubyLynx validation: 44 tx_correctness_suite tests ✓ (34 existing + 10 new: 6 concurrency stress, 2 reason code exhaustion, 2 kill-switch/pause pipeline). Also verified: 25 adversarial tests (mission_safety_adversarial) ✓, 20 proptest (proptest_mission_loop) ✓, 25 proptest (proptest_tx_idempotency) ✓, 15 proptest (proptest_idempotency_resume) ✓, 66 unit (tx_idempotency) ✓ = 195 total cross-module tests. New concurrency tests: concurrent_commit_determinism, concurrent_compensation_determinism, concurrent_idempotency_checks_consistent, concurrent_resume_reconstruction_determinism, concurrent_tx_key_computation_determinism, concurrent_mixed_tx_non_interference.  {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","title":"[mission][H11] Deterministic e2e scenario matrix for tx run/rollback flows","description":"## Objective
Build a deterministic end-to-end scenario matrix that proves tx behavior in realistic multi-pane operations.

## Scope
- E2E matrix: nominal commit, policy denial, reservation conflict, approval timeout, mid-commit failure, auto-rollback success, rollback partial, forced rollback, pause/resume.
- Scenario fixtures and scripts for reproducibility.
- Per-scenario rich logs + machine-readable pass/fail outputs.

## Deliverables
- E2E harness scripts and fixtures.
- Scenario result bundle format with timeline and verdict summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E suite covers all critical transaction states and failure classes.
2. Every scenario emits machine-readable verdicts plus rich timeline logs.
3. Assertions validate final system state consistency after commit/rollback.
4. Scripts are deterministic and rerunnable with fixed seeds/config.
5. E2E outputs include concise operator summaries and deep forensic artifacts.
6. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_e2e_matrix.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_e2e_matrix_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
7. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: this is the proof layer that tx semantics work in real orchestration conditions, not just unit abstractions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:34.913560Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:31:15.657944Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:31:15.657885Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mission","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
